title: CAR SEAT PROGRAM - 211info
text: BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seat checks with a certified car seat technician as well as installation. Primary caregivers may also purchase a car seat. Child must be present to fit the car seat correctly. 207 NE Park Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Child must be present for service Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 426-9411 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 426-9411 [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for appointment FEES: Car seats are provided on a sliding scale based on income. Priority is given to low income families. Households at or below 100% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) pay $10 for an infant or convertible car seat, and $5 for a booster. Households making 100-200% FPL pay half of the cost of the car seat. Households above 200% FPL may purchase a car seat at cost. Car seat checks are available for free. DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Wallowa County SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 8am-4:30pm, Friday 8am-noon Services: Child Passenger Safety Seat Inspections Child Passenger Safety Seats Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - HARVEST SHARE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTION MATERIAL GOODS - BABY RESOURCES SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - MINI MOUNTAINEERS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES
